untranslatable_reason: Signal requires regime detection (TRENDING vs RANGING) based on ADX crossovers and multi-day ADX rise patterns (3 of 4 days), and exit logic based on 5-day ADX differences. These multi-day pattern matching and regime classification operations are not directly expressible using the allowed operators (crosses_above, crosses_below, >, <, >=, <=, ==, is). The signal is fundamentally a regime classifier rather than a simple entry/exit rule, and requires conditional logic over rolling windows that exceeds the scope of the three-field condition structure.
translation_notes: This signal (from Kaufman/Ruggiero) is designed to classify market regimes (trending vs consolidating) rather than generate directional trades. Entry condition 4 ('ADX rises above 10 on 3 of 4 days') and exit condition ('5-day difference in ADX less than 0') require lookback and aggregation logic not supported by the allowed operators. A full implementation would require custom regime detection logic outside the standard backtest rule engine. Consider translating this into separate TRENDING_UP/TRENDING_DOWN regime detectors with explicit state machines.
